app.title: Minecraft Item Doll Generator
app.description: Combine Minecraft items, player skins, and server model declarations in the browser, then download the result as a resource-pack zip.
app.hero.body:
Enter the item ID (you can see it with F3+H), then upload a 64x64 player skin. Next, choose the doll preset you want to generate, and finally click "Generate ZIP" to download it.
If your server uses a resource pack, you can upload the server's item model mapping file with prior agreement so the result stays compatible with the server pack.
Everything happens fully in the browser, and no data is uploaded anywhere.
app.list.title: Item Configuration List
app.error.unknown: An unknown error occurred. Please try again later.
component.locale.label: Language
component.row.skin_upload.drag_hint: Or drag a skin file here
component.row.model_preset.dialog_hint: Pick the preset that best matches the item's pose
component.row.model_preset.dialog_empty: No presets are available right now.
component.row.override.dialog_desc: This JSON must include a fallback that points to the vanilla model, which means it must contain "{modelRef}".
component.row.override.placeholder: Paste or edit the server item mapping JSON here.
model.type.holding_item.desc: In first-person view, the player can see the item being hugged by the doll.
model.type.holding_item_reversed.desc: In first-person view, the player can see the back of the doll's head. If you use mods such as locomotion that may change first-person item rendering, this preset can help you see the item being hugged by the doll.
model.type.hugging_tool.desc: Suitable for poses where the character hugs a long tool or weapon with both arms. The hugging position sits slightly below the middle of the tool.
